title: leanstral prover — a proof backend, not just a model checker
manager: The verification era compiles machines out to finite-state model checkers (nuXmv, SPIN, Storm) and makes them agree. This is the research spike into a fundamentally different backend: a Lean-based theorem prover instead of a bounded state explorer. A model checker says 'no counterexample found within the bound'; a prover can say 'no counterexample exists, and here is the deductive argument for why.' The deliverable is a verdict plus a prototype — is the prover path worth its considerable tooling weight, and for which properties does machine-checked proof actually beat the model checkers we already bridge to, rather than just costing more.
cs: research/spike, not a shipped verb; targets properties that resist bounded MC (unbounded parameters, inductive invariants); rides the differential-agreement harness so a prover verdict is cross-checked against the --via backends; open question is the lowering from the IR into prover terms and how much proof obligation discharges automatically.
